+
+def aggregate(records: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    by_system: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = defaultdict(list)
+    for record in records:
+        by_system[record["system"]].append(record)
+
+    def mean(rows: list[dict[str, Any]], getter: Any) -> float:
+        values = [float(getter(row)) for row in rows]
+        return sum(values) / len(values) if values else 0.0
+
+    systems = {}
+    for system in SYSTEMS:
+        rows = by_system.get(system, [])
+        systems[system] = {
+            "attempts": len(rows),
+            "observed_repair_success_rate": mean(rows, lambda row: row["observed_repair_success"]),
+            "treatment_compliance_rate": mean(rows, lambda row: row["treatment_audit"]["passed"]),
+            "nonempty_patch_rate": mean(rows, lambda row: row["patch_bytes"] > 0),
+            "diff_check_rate": mean(rows, lambda row: row["diff_check_passed"]),
+            "successful_test_observation_rate": mean(rows, lambda row: any(item["passed"] for item in row["test_observations"])),
+            "reference_source_file_hit_rate": mean(rows, lambda row: row["alignment"]["reference_source_file_hit"]),
+            "mean_reference_source_file_recall": mean(rows, lambda row: row["alignment"]["reference_source_file_recall"]),
+            "mean_old_side_region_recall": mean(rows, lambda row: row["alignment"]["old_side_region_recall"]),
+            "mean_tool_calls": mean(rows, lambda row: row["tool_call_count"]),
+            "mean_wall_time_ms": mean(rows, lambda row: row["wall_time_ms"]),
+            "mean_input_tokens": mean(rows, lambda row: row["usage"].get("input_tokens") or 0),
+            "mean_output_tokens": mean(rows, lambda row: row["usage"].get("output_tokens") or 0),
+            "total_reported_cost_usd": sum(float(row.get("reported_total_cost_usd") or 0) for row in rows),
+        }
+    return systems
+
+
+def write_report(output: Path, summary: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
+    lines = [
+        "# Graph Agentic Repair Development Pilot",
+        "",
+        "Development-only; three already-excluded repositories; one attempt per treatment and repository.",
+        "Reference overlap is diagnostic and is not hidden-test correctness.",
+        "",
+        "| System | Attempts | Observed success | Treatment compliance | Test observed | Source-file hit | Region recall | Tool calls | Wall time (s) | Input tokens | Output tokens | Cost (USD) |",
+        "| --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: |",
+    ]
+    for system in SYSTEMS:
+        row = summary["systems"][system]
+        lines.append(
+            f"| {system} | {row['attempts']} | {row['observed_repair_success_rate']:.3f} | "
+            f"{row['treatment_compliance_rate']:.3f} | {row['successful_test_observation_rate']:.3f} | "
+            f"{row['reference_source_file_hit_rate']:.3f} | {row['mean_old_side_region_recall']:.3f} | "
+            f"{row['mean_tool_calls']:.1f} | {row['mean_wall_time_ms']/1000:.1f} | "
+            f"{row['mean_input_tokens']:.1f} | {row['mean_output_tokens']:.1f} | "
+            f"{row['total_reported_cost_usd']:.4f} |"
+        )
+    lines += [
+        "",
+        "`observed success` requires a non-empty clean patch, treatment compliance, a reference source-file hit, and at least one successful test command observed in the agent transcript.",
+        "",

+    (output / "scoreboard.md").write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")

+def execute(
+    manifest: dict[str, Any],
+    output: Path,
+    *,
+    claude_binary: Path,
+    graph_binary: Path,
+    cbm_binary: Path,
+    model: str,
+    max_budget_usd: float,
+    timeout_seconds: int,
+) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    records = []
+    for case in manifest["cases"]:
+        for system in case["system_order"]:
+            print(
+                f"agentic pilot case {case['execution_index']}/3 system={system}",

+                flush=True,
+            )
+            records.append(
+                execute_one(
+                    case=case,
+                    system=system,
+                    output=output,
+                    claude_binary=claude_binary,
+                    graph_binary=graph_binary,
+                    cbm_binary=cbm_binary,
+                    model=model,
+                    max_budget_usd=max_budget_usd,
+                    timeout_seconds=timeout_seconds,
+                )
+            )
+    systems = aggregate(records)
+    summary = {
+        "schema_version": 1,

+def executable_binding(path: Path, arguments: list[str]) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    resolved = path.resolve(strict=True)
+    if not os.access(resolved, os.X_OK):
+        raise PilotError(f"executable is not runnable: {resolved}")
+    process = run_command([str(resolved), *arguments], timeout=30)
+    if process.returncode != 0:
+        raise PilotError(f"version probe failed: {resolved}")
+    first = (process.stdout + process.stderr).decode("utf-8", "replace").splitlines()
+    return {
+        "sha256": sha256_file(resolved),
+        "version_first_line": first[0] if first else "",
+    }
+
+
+def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__)
+    parser.add_argument("command", choices=("prepare", "run", "all"))
+    parser.add_argument("--output", type=Path, required=True)
+    parser.add_argument("--manifest", type=Path, default=DEVELOPMENT / "manifest.json")
+    parser.add_argument("--repository-catalog", type=Path, default=DEVELOPMENT / "repository-catalog.json")
+    parser.add_argument("--judgments", type=Path, default=DEVELOPMENT / "judgments.json")
+    parser.add_argument("--claude-binary", type=Path, required=True)
+    parser.add_argument("--graph-binary", type=Path, required=True)
+    parser.add_argument("--cbm-binary", type=Path, required=True)
+    parser.add_argument("--model", default="opus")
+    parser.add_argument("--max-budget-usd", type=float, default=5.0)
+    parser.add_argument("--timeout-seconds", type=int, default=1800)
+    return parser
+
+
+def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
+    args = build_parser().parse_args(argv)
+    if args.max_budget_usd <= 0 or args.timeout_seconds <= 0:
+        print("error: budget and timeout must be positive", file=sys.stderr)
+        return 2
+    try:
+        bindings = {
+            "model": args.model,
+            "claude": executable_binding(args.claude_binary, ["--version"]),
+            "entire_graph": executable_binding(args.graph_binary, ["--version"]),
+            "cbm": executable_binding(args.cbm_binary, ["--version"]),
+            "runner_sha256": sha256_file(Path(__file__).resolve()),
+            "development_manifest_sha256": sha256_file(args.manifest.resolve(strict=True)),
+            "development_catalog_sha256": sha256_file(args.repository_catalog.resolve(strict=True)),
+            "development_judgments_sha256": sha256_file(args.judgments.resolve(strict=True)),
+        }
+        if args.command in {"prepare", "all"}:

+        if args.command in {"run", "all"}:

+                graph_binary=args.graph_binary.resolve(strict=True),
+                cbm_binary=args.cbm_binary.resolve(strict=True),

+        else:

+def git(path: Path, *arguments: str) -> str:

+        text=True,
+        capture_output=True,
+        check=True,
+        env={
+            "PATH": "/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin",
+            "GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL": "/dev/null",
+            "GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM": "1",
+            "LANG": "C",
+            "LC_ALL": "C",
+        },
+    )
+    return process.stdout.strip()

+def repository(tmp_path: Path) -> tuple[Path, str, str]:
+    path = tmp_path / "repo"
+    path.mkdir()
+    git(path, "init", "--quiet")

+    git(path, "config", "user.name", "Pilot")

+    git(path, "add", "main.py")
+    git(path, "commit", "--quiet", "-m", "base")
+    base = git(path, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
+    (path / "main.py").write_text("def answer():\n    return 2\n")
+    git(path, "add", "main.py")
+    git(path, "commit", "--quiet", "-m", "fix")
+    return path, base, git(path, "rev-parse", "HEAD")

content.text: Round 1 is running concurrently: native Claude file tools on the small repository, Entire Graph on the medium repository, and CBM on the large repository. All three reached the live agent loop without configuration or permission failures; no attempt has completed yet.

All three round-1 Opus processes are healthy after roughly four minutes. The controller is still enforcing separate base-only checkouts and treatment-specific tool availability; none has timed out or exited early.

Round 1 remains in active investigation/edit/test work; no controller errors or early failures have appeared. The large-repository CBM arm is expected to be the slowest because its first call includes building the persistent index, which is being charged and retained as part of the native workflow.

The first completed attempt produced a clean, reference-aligned patch with full reference source-file coverage, but Opus exhausted the 40-turn cap before running tests, so the harness correctly recorded it as unsuccessful. I’m letting the other two round-1 attempts finish before deciding whether the cap needs a protocol-wide increase; I won’t silently reclassify the incomplete run.

The large-repository CBM attempt also reached the correct source area and produced a clean patch, but it hit the same 40-turn ceiling and used shell `grep` once after 13 native CBM calls. The harness correctly failed both completion and treatment compliance. This is useful: the initial cap is too short for repair work, and prompt-only fallback prevention is insufficient; I’ll harden both before accepting a result.

content.text: I stopped the pilot after round 1 rather than spend six more attempts under a flawed cap. Two completed arms both reached the reference source and produced clean patches, but both exhausted 40 turns; one also bypassed the intended CBM-only discovery rule through shell `grep`. I’m preserving those aborted records as protocol-development evidence, increasing the cap uniformly, and replacing audit-only fallback detection with command-level permission restrictions before restarting from clean checkouts.

content.text: The hardened protocol is committed and pushed as `a3dd91c`. A live permission canary confirmed that Entire Graph remained callable while shell `grep` was actually denied and recorded. The replacement run now uses 80 turns per arm, the same $5 cap, clean regenerated checkouts, and the balanced three-round schedule.

The replacement round reached all three live agents cleanly. The restricted Graph/CBM arms are operating with native retrieval plus targeted `Read`; any attempted shell fallback will now be denied rather than merely detected afterward.
Round 1 is still in repair work with no controller errors, timeouts, or early exits. The turn increase is behaving as intended so far; none of the agents has reached its cap.
Five-minute update: all three round-1 attempts are still active. The first-run cap failure has not recurred, and the controller has not recorded any treatment escape or runtime failure.

Round 1 remains healthy but long-running, which is expected for actual repairs with build/test work rather than one-shot ranking. I’m continuing to watch the hard 30-minute per-attempt ceiling and the $5 per-attempt cap.

Round 1 exposed a second harness issue before any comparative result: the stricter policy successfully blocked fallback search, but it also denied legitimate repository-specific test launchers (`npx vitest` and local compiler paths). The Graph arm then exited without a patch and the CBM arm produced an unverified patch. I’m stopping this run too; letting six more attempts proceed would measure my permission list, not the products.

+    monkeypatch.setenv("PATH", "/usr/bin:/bin")
+    environment = pilot.runtime_environment("/runtime/bin:/manager/bin")
+    assert environment["PATH"] == "/runtime/bin:/manager/bin:/usr/bin:/bin"
+    assert environment["CI"] == "1"
+    assert environment["GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT"] == "0"
+
+
+def test_bootstrap_installs_once_then_clones_identical_checkout(tmp_path: Path):
+    source, _base, fix = repository(tmp_path)
+    (source / "pnpm-lock.yaml").write_text("lockfileVersion: '9.0'\n")
+    git(source, "add", "pnpm-lock.yaml")
+    git(source, "commit", "--quiet", "-m", "lock dependencies")
+    head = git(source, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
+    checkouts = {"file-tools": source}
+    for system in ("entire-graph", "cbm"):
+        destination = tmp_path / system
+        pilot.run_command(["/bin/cp", "-cR", str(source), str(destination)])
+        checkouts[system] = destination
+    manager_bin = tmp_path / "bin"
+    manager_bin.mkdir()
+    fake_pnpm = manager_bin / "pnpm"
+    fake_pnpm.write_text("#!/bin/sh\nmkdir -p node_modules/frozen\nprintf installed > node_modules/frozen/marker\n")
+    fake_pnpm.chmod(0o755)
+    manifest = {
+        "cases": [
+            {
+                "case_binding": "opaque-case",
+                "execution_index": 1,
+                "base_commit": head,
+                "checkouts": {key: str(value) for key, value in checkouts.items()},
+            }
+        ]
+    }
+
+    summary = pilot.bootstrap_dependencies(
+        manifest,
+        tmp_path / "output",
+        runtime_path_prefix=str(manager_bin),
+    )
+
+    assert summary["records"][0]["status"] == "installed_and_cloned"
+    for checkout in checkouts.values():
+        assert (checkout / "node_modules/frozen/marker").read_text() == "installed"
+        assert git(checkout, "rev-parse", "HEAD") == head

content.text: CBM on repo 1 exited after 11.9 minutes with a source-aligned six-file patch, but it did not count as success: its compile check was denied because the command piped output through `tail`. That exposes a remaining protocol flaw—our anti-discovery shell guard is also blocking normal test-output filtering. I’m preserving the run, not changing rules mid-round; I’ll correct that before treating any comparison as product evidence.

content.text: Entire Graph on repo 3 exited at its $5 cap with a three-file, fully precise patch hitting two-thirds of reference source files and 46% of reference regions. It still did not qualify as completed, and the record exposed a second harness bug: a shell file-existence check containing the word `mocha` was misclassified as a test. I’ll fix both protocol defects before any rerun or ranking.

content.text: The first full rotation is complete and retained, but it is diagnostic—not rankable. I’m correcting the two proven harness defects now: normal build/test pipelines may filter their output, while discovery fallbacks are audited after execution; and test detection will require an actual command invocation rather than merely seeing a test-runner name in a path or echo. I’ll test and push that correction before deciding whether another paid run is justified.

+# Agentic repair diagnostic — 2026-07-17

+This three-repository, three-treatment development run exercised the real
+Claude Opus edit-and-test loop. It is diagnostic evidence, not a product
+ranking. It did not inspect or authorize V8 material.

content.text: Summarize the results until this point

The agent-native benchmark is built, tested, pushed, and in draft PR [#34](https://github.com/entirehq/brain-bench/pull/34). The corrected rerun has not started yet.

### Diagnostic run results

Three development repositories × three Claude Opus treatments, with real discovery, editing, and testing:

| Treatment | Patches | Reference-source hits | Mean source recall | Mean region recall | Successful test runs | Completed repairs |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Entire Graph | 2/3 | 2/3 | **0.556** | **0.205** | 0/3 | 0/3 |
| Built-in file tools | **3/3** | 2/3 | 0.500 | 0.107 | **1/3** | 0/3 |
| CBM | 2/3 | 1/3 | 0.333 | 0.064 | 0/3 | 0/3 |

Entire Graph produced the best reference alignment, including a precise two-file patch on one repository and a three-file patch covering 46% of reference regions on another. However, it did not complete a tested repair within the limits, so this does not establish a win.

Four of nine attempts timed out. The other five reported $18.58 combined; timeout costs were not returned by Claude CLI.

### Why the run is not rankable

Two harness defects affected the comparison:

- Shell restrictions blocked legitimate output filtering, including a CBM compile command using `tail` and an Entire Graph search piped through `head`.
- Test detection incorrectly counted a file-existence check containing the word `mocha` as a test.

Both are fixed, and the suite now passes 42/42 tests. The affected run is permanently labeled diagnostic rather than retroactively rescored as valid evidence.

Evidence is recorded in [diagnostic-run-20260717.md](/private/tmp/brain-bench-graph-agentic-pilot/competitive-eval/agentic-repair-pilot/diagnostic-run-20260717.md) and its [canonical metrics JSON](/private/tmp/brain-bench-graph-agentic-pilot/competitive-eval/agentic-repair-pilot/diagnostic-run-20260717.json).

Current status:

- Corrected clean rerun: not started yet.
- Draft PR: [Brain-Bench #34](https://github.com/entirehq/brain-bench/pull/34), stacked on PR #29’s branch.
- PR #29 itself was not modified or retargeted.
- Entire Graph and Entire Brain were not changed.
- V8 authorization remains false; no V8 identities or evaluation contents were inspected.

data=json.loads((root/'competitive-eval/graph-v8/graph-toolchain-closure.json').read_text())
for relative, descriptor in data['source_closure']['local_sources'].items():
    observed=hashlib.sha256((root/relative).read_bytes()).hexdigest()
    if observed != descriptor['sha256']:
        print(relative)
        print('expected', descriptor['sha256'])
        print('observed', observed)

content.text:
Next is the fresh corrected three-repository retry.

One caveat: Brain-Bench PR [#34](https://github.com/entirehq/brain-bench/pull/34) is red because its base branch already contains two stale V8 toolchain hashes. That failure is unrelated to the pilot. Fixing it would enter the protected V8/PR #29 scope, so I recommend leaving it alone and documenting it as inherited.

The sequence is:

1. Run the corrected 3×3 Opus pilot in entirely fresh checkouts.
2. Produce valid quality, latency, token, cost, patch-alignment, and test-completion metrics.
3. Commit and push the evidence to PR #34.
4. If Entire Graph does not win, trace the losing attempts and implement general product improvements in Entire Graph.
5. Test and push those improvements through the already-green Entire Graph [PR #43](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/pull/43), then rerun once.

Expected retry: roughly 60–90 minutes and likely $20–35, with a hard theoretical ceiling of $45. V8 remains untouched.
Do it

Starting the clean corrected retry now. I’ll use fresh base-only checkouts, identical dependency trees, the same Opus budgets and balanced rotations, and I’ll report progress at least every five minutes.

content.text: The Graph attempt completed a source-aligned, tested patch, but it naturally used targeted `grep` after Graph had identified the files, so the exclusive-treatment audit rejected it. That confirms a deeper alignment issue: real agents use Graph or CBM alongside ordinary file tools, not as total replacements. I’m stopping the remaining spend now and will change the arms to “normal agent tools,” “normal tools + Graph,” and “normal tools + CBM,” requiring actual product use but not forbidding follow-up inspection.

-output. Shell file-search fallbacks are captured and make the treatment
-noncompliant unless they occur only as output filters after a build or test
-launcher in the same command.

+shell tools available to the baseline. Product use is required and recorded,
+but follow-up Grep, Glob, Read, or shell inspection remains available. This
+matches how coding agents use an additional retrieval product in practice.

content.text: The native repo-3 attempt hit the $5 cap with a highly aligned patch, but its reported “test observations” are false positives caused by test-runner names inside quoted `grep` patterns. I’ve isolated the cause: regex cannot parse shell quoting reliably. I’m replacing it with shell-token parsing before final scoring; this remains a scorer-only correction and will distinguish actual launch commands from quoted text.

input.input: const r = await tools.exec_command({cmd:"rg -n 'TEST_COMMAND_RE' competitive-eval/tools/run_graph_agentic_repair_pilot.py competitive-eval/tools/test_run_graph_agentic_repair_pilot.py","workdir":"/private/tmp/brain-bench-graph-agentic-pilot","yield_time_ms":10000,"max_output_tokens":3000}); text(r.output);

const patch = "*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /private/tmp/brain-bench-graph-agentic-pilot/competitive-eval/tools/run_graph_agentic_repair_pilot.py
@@
 import os
 import re
+import shlex
 import shutil
@@
-TEST_COMMAND_RE = re.compile(
-    r"(?:^|(?:&&|\|\||;|\|)\s*)(?:timeout\s+\d+\s+)?(?:"
-    r"pytest|python\s+-m\s+pytest|go\s+test|cargo\s+test|ctest|"
-    r"npm\s+(?:run\s+)?test[^\s]*|pnpm\s+(?:run\s+)?test[^\s]*|yarn\s+test[^\s]*|"
-    r"npx(?:\s+--no-install)?\s+(?:vitest|mocha)|(?:\./)?node_modules/\.bin/(?:vitest|mocha)|"
-    r"vitest|mocha|make\s+(?:check|test)|"
-    r"(?:\./|/tmp/)(?!configure(?:\s|$))[^\s;&|]+|"
-    r"mvn(?:w)?\s+[^;&|]*test|gradle(?:w)?\s+[^;&|]*test)",
-    re.IGNORECASE,
-)
 class PilotError(ValueError):
@@
 def bash_command(tool: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
@@
     return value if isinstance(value, str) else ""
+
+
+def shell_segments(command: str) -> list[list[str]]:
+    """Tokenize shell command boundaries without treating quoted pipes as syntax."""
+
+    lexer = shlex.shlex(command, posix=True, punctuation_chars=";&|")
+    lexer.whitespace_split = True
+    lexer.commenters = ""
+    try:
+        tokens = list(lexer)
+    except ValueError:
+        return []
+    segments: list[list[str]] = []
+    current: list[str] = []
+    for token in tokens:
+        if token and all(character in ";&|" for character in token):
+            if current:
+                segments.append(current)
+                current = []
+        else:
+            current.append(token)
+    if current:
+        segments.append(current)
+    return segments
+
+
+def segment_invokes_test(tokens: list[str]) -> bool:
+    if not tokens:
+        return False
+    if tokens[0] == "timeout" and len(tokens) >= 3:
+        tokens = tokens[2:]
+    if not tokens:
+        return False
+    executable = tokens[0]
+    basename = Path(executable).name.lower()
+    arguments = [value.lower() for value in tokens[1:]]
+    if basename == "pytest":
+        return True
+    if basename in {"python", "python3"} and arguments[:2] == ["-m", "pytest"]:
+        return True
+    if basename in {"go", "cargo"} and arguments[:1] == ["test"]:
+        return True
+    if basename == "ctest":
+        return True
+    if basename in {"npm", "pnpm", "yarn"}:
+        script = arguments[1] if arguments[:1] == ["run"] and len(arguments) > 1 else (arguments[0] if arguments else "")
+        return script.startswith("test")
+    if basename == "npx":
+        runner = next((value for value in arguments if not value.startswith("-")), "")
+        return runner in {"ava", "jest", "mocha", "vitest"}
+    if basename in {"ava", "jest", "mocha", "vitest"}:
+        return True
+    if basename == "make" and any(value in {"check", "test", "tests"} for value in arguments):
+        return True
+    if basename in {"mvn", "mvnw", "gradle", "gradlew"} and any("test" in value for value in arguments):
+        return True
+    if executable.startswith("./node_modules/.bin/"):
+        return basename in {"ava", "jest", "mocha", "vitest"}
+    if executable.startswith(("./", "/tmp/", "/private/tmp/")):
+        return basename != "configure"
+    return False
+
+
+def is_test_command(command: str) -> bool:
+    return any(segment_invokes_test(segment) for segment in shell_segments(command))
@@
-        if not command or not TEST_COMMAND_RE.search(command):
+        if not command or not is_test_command(command):
*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

+
+def rescore(output: Path, graph_binary: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    """Recompute treatment and verification metrics without mutating raw records."""
+
+    updated_records = []
+    provenance = []
+    for record_path in sorted((output / "records").glob("case-*/*/record.json")):
+        record = artifacts.read_canonical_json(record_path, expected_type=dict).document
+        transcript_path = record_path.with_name("transcript.jsonl")
+        parsed = parse_stream(transcript_path.read_bytes())
+        audit = audit_treatment(record["system"], parsed["tools"], graph_binary)
+        tests = test_tool_observations(parsed["tools"])
+        rescored = dict(record)
+        rescored["treatment_audit"] = audit
+        rescored["test_observations"] = tests
+        rescored["observed_repair_success"] = (
+            record["patch_bytes"] > 0
+            and record["diff_check_passed"]
+            and audit["passed"]
+            and record["alignment"]["reference_source_file_hit"]
+            and any(row["passed"] for row in tests)
+            and not record["timed_out"]
+            and record["returncode"] == 0
+        )
+        updated_records.append(rescored)
+        provenance.append(
+            {
+                "case_binding": record["case_binding"],
+                "system": record["system"],
+                "source_record_sha256": sha256_file(record_path),
+                "source_transcript_sha256": sha256_file(transcript_path),
+                "treatment_audit": audit,
+                "test_observations": [
+                    {
+                        "command_sha256": row["command_sha256"],
+                        "observed": row["observed"],
+                        "passed": row["passed"],
+                    }
+                    for row in tests
+                ],
+                "observed_repair_success": rescored["observed_repair_success"],
+            }
+        )
+    if len(updated_records) != 9:
+        raise PilotError("rescore requires all nine immutable attempt records")
+    source_summary = output / "summary.json"
+    summary = {
+        "schema_version": 1,
+        "suite": SUITE,
+        "stage": "complete_posthoc_parser_rescore",
+        "scorer_sha256": sha256_file(Path(__file__).resolve()),
+        "source_summary_sha256": sha256_file(source_summary),
+        "systems": aggregate(updated_records),
+        "attempts": provenance,
+        "claim_boundary": {
+            "agent_outcomes_measured": True,
+            "hidden_or_upstream_acceptance_measured": False,
+            "reference_overlap_is_task_correctness": False,
+            "development_only": True,
+            "raw_records_mutated": False,
+        },
+    }
+    canonical_write(output / "rescored-summary.json", summary)
+    write_report(output, summary, "rescored-scoreboard.md")
+    return summary

+# Agentic augmentation development run — 2026-07-17
+
+This run compares the workflow a coding agent actually uses: normal Claude
+Code file and shell tools alone, with Entire Graph added, or with CBM added.
+All nine Claude Opus attempts used isolated base-only checkouts, equal locked
+dependencies, the same issue prompt, a $5 cap, an 80-turn cap, and a 30-minute
+wall-time cap. Product arms had to invoke their product, but could continue
+with ordinary Grep, Glob, Read, and shell tools.
+
+This is development evidence, not confirmatory evidence. Reference-patch
+overlap is diagnostic rather than hidden correctness, and no V8 identities or
+outcomes were inspected.
+
+| Treatment | Repair success | Source hit | Mean source recall | Mean region recall | Verification | Tool calls | Wall time | Output tokens | Cost |
+|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
+| Entire Graph | 0/3 | 2/3 | 0.500 | 0.094 | 2/3 | 56.3 | 1028.2 s | 60,599 | $12.8449 |
+| File tools | 1/3 | 3/3 | 0.722 | 0.235 | 2/3 | 48.3 | 834.7 s | 53,970 | $12.1981 |
+| CBM | 1/3 | 3/3 | 0.722 | 0.209 | 2/3 | 47.7 | 686.2 s | 42,654 | $10.8513 |
+
+Entire Graph lost the primary outcome and every reported retrieval-alignment
+and efficiency aggregate. All treatments tied on non-empty clean patches,
+treatment compliance, and successful-verification rate.
+
+The three Graph outcomes explain the aggregate:
+
+- One attempt found the right source and passed targeted tests, but exhausted
+  the $5 budget before returning the final structured answer.
+- One produced the expected source-aligned repair and ran compiled
+  reproductions, but failed the structured-output contract after 78 turns.
+- One selected the wrong subsystem and had zero reference-source and region
+  overlap, then also failed structured output.
+
+The raw records were not rewritten. A separate rescore used quote-aware shell
+tokenization so direct reproduction binaries count as verification while test
+runner names inside quoted search patterns do not. The JSON artifact binds the
+raw and rescored summaries plus a sorted hash list for all 36 transcripts,
+patches, records, and stderr files.
*** End Patch";

content.text: The refinement is pushed as `332c599`. On the three burned development repositories, the new agent output is 8.8–10.7 KiB instead of nearly exhausting the 16 KiB JSON budget, while still returning all twenty locators. It now shows ten distinct files before repeats; in the largest repository the causal runner, scheduler, service, and runner test are at ranks 1, 2, 9, and 13, while the misleading UI file is pushed to rank 17 on the initial query. I’m validating the follow-up query and full repository checks before changing the pilot harness and rerunning.

const patch = "*** Begin Patch

@@

+        treatment = f"""Treatment: use the normal Claude Code file tools plus Entire Graph. Invoke `{graph_binary} search --repo . --query \"...\" --format agent` through Bash at least once, then use Graph, Grep, Glob, Read, and normal shell tools in whatever combination is most useful. The agent format preserves ranked locations and focused source within the product's default 16 KiB context budget. Start Graph with the product defaults (top 20, worktree view), reformulate queries as needed, and escalate depth only when useful."""
@@

@@

content.text: The full Entire Graph check now passes (format, vet, race tests, and build). I’ve also updated and pushed the development pilot harness as Brain-Bench commit `e9b8cb4`; it now requires the compact agent format while leaving ordinary agent file tools available. The follow-up query still ranks the status model first, but now keeps the causal runner at 4/12 and scheduler at 13 while limiting the UI subsystem to 5/16—better coverage, though not a guarantee against agent drift. Next is the paid three-arm rerun on the same burned repositories.

It ties baseline on success (1/3), beats CBM (0/3), and has the lowest product-arm cost/output tokens, yet baseline remains better on judged-region recall, source recall, latency, output tokens, and cost. +# Agent-format development run — 2026-07-18
+
+This rerun evaluates the general Entire Graph product changes at commit
+`332c599`: compact agent-facing search output, ranked snippet allocation, and a
+distinct-file discovery reserve. The same three already-excluded development
+repositories and the same realistic augmentation protocol were used. All nine
+Claude Opus attempts had isolated base-only checkouts, equal locked
+dependencies, normal file and shell tools, a $5 cap, an 80-turn cap, and a
+30-minute wall-time cap.
+
+This is development evidence, not confirmatory evidence. Reference-patch
+overlap is diagnostic rather than hidden correctness. No V8 identity,
+catalog, ranking, scoring, or publication authorization changed.
+
+| Treatment | Repair success | Source hit | Mean source recall | Mean region recall | Verification | Tool calls | Wall time | Output tokens | Cost |
+|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
+| Entire Graph | 1/3 | 3/3 | 0.611 | 0.145 | 2/3 | 48.7 | 818.8 s | 49,865 | $10.7653 |
+| File tools | 1/3 | 3/3 | 0.722 | 0.372 | 2/3 | 48.7 | 726.4 s | 45,535 | $9.4930 |
+| CBM | 0/3 | 3/3 | 0.722 | 0.209 | 2/3 | 50.3 | 826.9 s | 50,601 | $11.6456 |
+
+Relative to the prior Graph run, observed success improved from 0/3 to 1/3,
+source hit from 2/3 to 3/3, source recall from 0.500 to 0.611, region recall
+from 0.094 to 0.145, tool calls from 56.3 to 48.7, wall time from 1028.2 to
+818.8 seconds, output tokens from 60,599 to 49,865, and total cost from
+$12.8449 to $10.7653.
+
+The product changes fixed the prior large-repository subsystem failure: Graph
+patched the causal automation service and its reference test instead of the UI
+subsystem, then returned structured output in 33 turns. That attempt still
+failed the protocol because it did not run a verification command. The median
+case found and tested the correct source but exhausted the $5 cap before final
+output. The smallest case was a complete success with both reference source
+files and five passing compiled reproductions.
+
+Entire Graph therefore improved materially and beat CBM on observed success,
+tool calls, latency, output tokens, and cost in this run. It did not beat the
+built-in file-tool baseline: success tied, while the baseline retained better
+source/region recall, latency, output-token, and cost aggregates.
+
+The raw records were not rewritten. The JSON artifact binds the raw and
+rescored summaries plus a sorted hash list for all 36 transcripts, patches,
+records, and stderr files.
*** End Patch";

content.text:
Implemented, tested, committed, pushed, and reran the full three-repository pilot.

Results:

| Treatment | Success | Region recall | Wall time | Output tokens | Cost |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Entire Graph | 1/3 | 0.145 | 818.8s | 49,865 | $10.77 |
| File tools | 1/3 | 0.372 | 726.4s | 45,535 | $9.49 |
| CBM | 0/3 | 0.209 | 826.9s | 50,601 | $11.65 |

Compared with the previous Graph run:

- Success: 0/3 → 1/3
- Source hits: 2/3 → 3/3
- Source recall: 0.500 → 0.611
- Region recall: 0.094 → 0.145
- Tool calls: 56.3 → 48.7
- Wall time: 1028s → 819s
- Output tokens: 60,599 → 49,865
- Cost: $12.84 → $10.77
- The large-repository wrong-subsystem failure was fixed.

Graph now beats CBM on success, tool calls, latency, output tokens, and cost. It still does not beat built-in file tools: success ties, while file tools retain better recall and efficiency.

The remaining two Graph failures were:

- Correct source and passing Vitest, but exhausted the $5 budget before final output.
- Correct causal service and reference test, but did not execute verification.

Entire Graph PR [#43](https://github.com/entireio/entire-graph/pull/43) is green and now includes commits `cb81d19` and `332c599`. Evidence is recorded in [agent-format-run-20260718.md](/private/tmp/brain-bench-graph-agentic-pilot/competitive-eval/agentic-repair-pilot/agent-format-run-20260718.md) and pushed to draft Brain-Bench PR #34 as `d95aecb`.

I also evaluated a worktree-listing latency optimization, found no measurable benefit, and discarded it rather than adding risk. No further paid run was started.
